key_insight: SoftVTBench shows that deformable-object manipulation must be judged not only by task completion but also by whether the robot stays physically safe throughout execution.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark gives robotics and solid-mechanics researchers a reproducible way to evaluate visuo-tactile policies under realistic deformation, slip, and drop constraints.
method: Builds a safety-aware benchmark in Isaac Sim with FEM-simulated deformable objects
Uses multi-view RGB, RGB tactile marker motion, proprioception, and language instructions
Defines Goal Success and stricter Safety Success using hidden FEM state thresholds
evidence_signal: Abstract results indicate that success-only evaluation overstates performance, and adding tactile sensing improved Safety Success from 21.4% to 35.6% on object-centric deformable tasks.
watch_item: Safety scores depend on calibrated object-specific deformation thresholds and privileged FEM state access, so results may be sensitive to simulation fidelity and threshold choice.
paper_title: SoftVTBench: A Safety-Aware Visuo-Tactile Benchmark for Physically Constrained Robotic Manipulation of Deformable Objects
